description: The central joint of the leg that enables bending, kneeling, and cushioned movement. The knee is the leg's hinge, transforming the rigid into the flexible. It allows us to kneel in reverence, to climb and descend, to absorb shock and adjust to terrain. A point of vulnerability and capability.
sound_symbolism: The word bends with purpose. 'Ku' begins with hard contact, the solid joint itself. 'Noa' grounds with presence, the pivot point where standing meets kneeling. A word as functional as the joint it names.
grammatical_notes: Refers to the joint specifically. In contexts of kneeling or genuflection, may appear with posture verbs. 'wi kunoa' (two knees / both knees) common in ritual contexts.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The knee enables sitting postures for meditation and the gesture of kneeling that appears across contemplative traditions. Its flexibility serves stillness.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Knees bear the weight of work—kneeling to plant, to harvest, to tend fire. They carry the memory of labor and reverence alike.
